question: US x Iran ceasefire by April 15?
narrative_context: Trump says Iran president asked for ceasefire (April 1, 2026)
Iran denies requesting ceasefire but says willing to accept with conditions (April 1, 2026)
Iran demands guaranteed ceasefire with security guarantees (April 1, 2026)
Trump's 15-point ceasefire plan rejected by Iran as 'unconditional surrender' (March 31, 2026)
Iran demands Strait of Hormuz sovereignty; US wants it open (March 25, 2026)
US-Israel war with Iran ongoing since February 28, 2026
evidence: Trump statement: Iran president asked for ceasefire; US willing if Hormuz is open (CNBC, April 1, 2026)
Iran Foreign Ministry: Iran 'willing to accept ceasefire with security guarantees' (Al Jazeera, April 1, 2026)
Negotiations active via Pakistani intermediaries (Axios, April 1, 2026)
Iran officially rejected Trump's 15-point ceasefire plan as demanding 'unconditional surrender' (CNBC, March 31, 2026)
Iran Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi: Iran not seeking ceasefire but 'end to war' (Al Jazeera, April 1, 2026)
Iran demands Strait of Hormuz sovereignty; Trump administration wants it open (CNBC, March 25, 2026)
Timeline: US-Israel airstrikes began February 28, 2026; 37 days of conflict so far
counter_evidence: Negotiations ARE occurring as of April 1, 2026 (latest); Iran expressed willingness to accept ceasefire with security guarantees; Trump administration appears willing to negotiate on Hormuz issue. However, fundamental positions remain far apart, and market still prices 34.5% by April 15 deadline.
thesis: Market prices 34.5% probability of US-Iran ceasefire by April 15, 2026. We estimate 8% (NO_UNDERPRICED). While negotiations have restarted as of April 1 with Iran expressing conditional willingness to accept ceasefire, the 13-day timeline is extremely tight for agreement on fundamental terms. Iran demands security guarantees and Hormuz sovereignty; Trump administration demands Hormuz reopened. Trump's 15-point plan was dismissed. Ceasefire requires clear public confirmation from both governments—Iran's 'willingness with conditions' and Trump's conditional offers fall short of agreed ceasefire status. Even if negotiators achieve breakthrough, formal announcement and implementation by April 15 is unlikely given 37 days of escalating conflict, mutual distrust, and non-negotiable position gaps. Market's 34.5% seems to overweight recent diplomatic engagement and underweight structural barriers to 13-day resolution.
x_thread: Market prices 34.5% chance of US-Iran ceasefire by April 15 (13 days away). We estimate 8%. Gap: 25.5pp. Why? Negotiations just restarted (April 1) but fundamental positions remain miles apart: Iran wants security guarantees + Hormuz control; Trump wants Hormuz open. Too much to resolve in 13 days.

Recent: Iran says it's willing to discuss ceasefire with conditions (April 1). Trump: open to talking if Hormuz opens. But Trump's 15-point plan was already rejected. Previous ceasefire talks have repeatedly stalled. 37 days into conflict. Formal ceasefire announcement by April 15 unlikely.
